intent: what is a loop, how to create a good loop, and loop creation best practices

content.critique: 1) Tighten intent alignment first. The draft answers 'what is a loop' and 'how to build one' well, but it narrows the topic to AI-agent orchestration almost immediately. If the search intent is broader, open with a plain-English definition of a loop first, then distinguish programming loops, workflow loops, and agent loops in one short section before committing to the agent-specific angle. 2) Improve grounding aggressively. Several claims sound more precise than the visible source set safely supports, especially the quantitative result about silent failures reaching 0.0% and some policy/verification conclusions attributed to [S2] and [S6]. Either quote exact findings more carefully, reduce precision, or add direct source-backed wording. Right now the article sometimes reads more certain than the citations justify. 3) Cut architecture-adjacent drift. Sections on execution boundaries, signed intents, governance, and contracts are useful, but they start to feel like adjacent agent-systems design rather than core loop creation advice. Compress them and keep the center of gravity on loop mechanics: iteration unit, validation, stop conditions, recovery, and observability. 4) Add one simpler 'how to create a good loop' example. The customer-record example is solid, but a minimal 5-step template or pseudocode snippet would make the advice more immediately actionable. 5) Sharpen best-practice prioritization. The list is good but long. Re-rank into must-have vs nice-to-have so readers leave with a clear hierarchy instead of an exhaustive catalog.
